events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON. Need parse scenario. Image shows evacuation decay test pump isolated. Microns @ isolation t=0 = 464. Microns @ t=10 min = 1824. Decay behavior indicates pressure rise from 464 to 1824 microns in 10 minutes. That is significant rise. In HVAC-R evacuation, acceptable decay typically <500 microns and rise less than ~500 microns over time (manufacturer spec often <1000 microns and no more than few hundred microns rise in 15-30 min). Here final 1824 microns > 1000, rise ~1360 microns in 10 min. Indicates leak, moisture, non-condensables, outgassing, poor vacuum. Since pump isolated, pressure rise due to leak or contamination. Component condition non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk medium? If refrigerant leak or moisture, high? Need assess. Leak means system cannot hold vacuum; potential refrigerant loss, environmental, compressor damage. Risk high maybe. Work stage tested/inspected. Percent complete maybe 80%? Evacuation incomplete. Value = 1824 (10-minute reading). Sound_source not asked, event_time 0, rate maybe microns/min = (1824-464)/10 = 136.0 microns/min. order empty. workable empty. findings: vacuum decay test failed; pressure rose from 464 to 1824 microns in 10 min; exceeds acceptable limits; likely leak/moisture/non-condensables. actions: locate and repair leak, replace drier, re-evacuate to specified microns, re-test decay, verify with leak detection. rationale: pressure rise after pump isolation indicates system not tight/dry; final reading >1000 microns and rise rate high. references: EPA 608 (proper evacuation and leak repair), mfr specs. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON. Let's craft.
